content:
# John Chapter 14 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_014_page_001.jpg, JHN_014_page_002.jpg

1. "Let not the heart of you be troubled; believe in the God, and in me believe.

2. In the house of the Father of me are many dwelling places; and if not, I would have told you, for I go to prepare a place for you.

3. And if I go and prepare a place for you, I come again and will take you to myself, that where I am, you also may be.

4. And where I go, you know the way."

5. Thomas says to him, "Lord, we do not know where you go. How are we able to know the way?"

6. The Yeshua says to him, "I am the way and the truth and the life; no one comes to the Father except through me.

7. If you have known me, you will know the Father of me also. And from now you know him and have seen him."

8. Philippos says to him, "Lord, show us the Father, and it suffices us."

9. The Yeshua says to him, "So long a time I am with you, and you have not known me, Philippos? The [one] having seen me has seen the Father. How do you say, 'Show us the Father'?

10. Do you not believe that I [am] in the Father and the Father is in me? The words that I say to you I do not speak from myself, but the Father abiding in me does the works of him.

11. Believe me that I [am] in the Father and the Father [is] in me; but if not, believe because of the works themselves.

12. Amen, amen, I say to you, the [one] believing in me, the works that I do, that [one] also will do, and greater than these he will do, because I go to the Father.

13. And whatever you ask in the name of me, this I will do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.

14. If you ask me anything in the name of me, I will do [it].

15. If you love me, keep the commandments of me.

16. And I will ask the Father, and another Advocate he will give you, that he may be with you to the age —

17. the Spirit of the truth, which the world is not able to receive, because it does not see it nor know it. You know it, because with you it abides and in you it will be.

18. I will not leave you orphans; I come to you.

19. Still a little [while] and the world no longer sees me, but you see me. Because I live, you also will live.

20. In that day you will know that I [am] in the Father of me, and you in me, and I in you.

21. The [one] having the commandments of me and keeping them, that [one] is the [one] loving me. And the [one] loving me will be loved by the Father of me, and I will love him and will manifest myself to him."

22. Ioudas — not the Iskariotes — says to him, "Lord, what has happened that to us you are about to manifest yourself and not to the world?"

23. The Yeshua answered and said to him, "If anyone loves me, the word of me he will keep, and the Father of me will love him, and to him we will come and dwelling with him we will make.

24. The [one] not loving me does not keep the words of me. And the word that you hear is not mine but [belongs] to the Father who sent me.

25. These things I have spoken to you while abiding with you.

26. But the Advocate, the Holy Spirit, which the Father will send in the name of me, that [one] will teach you all things and will remind you of all things that I said to you.

27. Peace I leave to you, the peace of me I give to you. Not as the world gives do I give to you. Let not the heart of you be troubled, neither let it be afraid.

28. You heard that I said to you, 'I go away and I come to you.' If you loved me, you would have rejoiced that I go to the Father, because the Father is greater than I.

29. And now I have told you before it happens, that when it happens you may believe.

30. No longer much will I speak with you, for the ruler of the world comes, and in me he has nothing.

31. But that the world may know that I love the Father, and just as the Father commanded me, thus I do. Rise, let us go from here."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.2 — "Many dwelling places" (μοναὶ πολλαί):** μονή means "dwelling place, room, abode" — not "mansions" (KJV). The word appears only here and in v.23, where Jesus promises to make his "dwelling" with the believer. The Father's house has room for all.

2. **v.6 — "I am the way and the truth and the life":** The sixth "I am" statement with predicate. Three nouns, one article — "the way" — suggesting that truth and life are functions of the way: Jesus is the way that is true and that leads to life. The exclusivity claim ("no one comes to the Father except through me") is absolute in John's theology.

3. **v.16 — "Another Advocate" (ἄλλον παράκλητον):** ἄλλος means "another of the same kind" (not ἕτερος, "another of a different kind"). The Advocate/Paraclete is "another" like Jesus — the same kind of divine presence continuing Jesus' ministry. παράκλητος can mean advocate, helper, comforter, or counselor.

4. **v.17 — "Spirit of the truth":** Sinaiticus reads the neuter pronoun (αὐτό) for the Spirit, since πνεῦμα is grammatically neuter in Greek. This does not imply impersonality — the Spirit is personal (teaching, reminding, testifying).

5. **v.28 — "The Father is greater than I":** One of the most debated verses in Christology. In context, Jesus speaks of his departure to the Father as a cause for joy — he returns to the one who sent him. "Greater" refers to the Father's position as sender, not to ontological superiority.

— EMET, AI Translator
